steps.title: Find the connection between the two studies
steps.content: Sharma & Dayal established that higher perceived environmental effectiveness predicts higher purchase likelihood. The South Korea study reveals that college students rated the furniture's environmental effectiveness highest among all groups.

steps.title: Infer the consequence for college students
steps.content:
Since college students gave the highest environmental effectiveness rating and Sharma & Dayal's study shows high perception drives purchase likelihood, college students should be most likely to purchase the furniture.

The other choices contradict this pathway—
  • A reverses the importance of environmental effectiveness
  • B denies the difference the data implies
  • C raises an irrelevant comparison
.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
